front.title: Terminology for Networking Infrastructure in the Internet of Agents

abstract.t:
The emergence of the Internet of Agents (IoA) introduces new requirements for interoperable collaboration among autonomous agents across heterogeneous networks, platforms, and administrative domains. Supporting such environments requires common understanding of infrastructure functions, collaboration mechanisms, semantic interaction, and trust-related concepts.
This document defines terminology for networking infrastructure in IoA environments, with a focus on Dynamic Multi-agent Secured Collaboration (DMSC). The terminology defined in this document is intended to support consistent usage across DMSC-related architecture, framework, and protocol specifications.
